title: 1985 Denver Mini Classic, Boys 10 - 11, Stage 3 Criterium Awards Ceremony

enrichment.summary: An awards ceremony announcer reads out the 6th through 2nd place finishers in the Boys 10-11 Stage 3 Criterium at the 1985 Denver Mini Classic cycling race. Kyle Pounds is called to receive 6th place, along with other young riders receiving ribbons, gift certificates from McDonald's, B&H Sports, and Denver Verspoke.
enrichment.speaker: other

claims.subject: Kyle Pounds
claims.claim: Kyle Pounds received fifth place ribbon and a gift certificate from McDonald's number 38.

claims.subject: Tim How
claims.claim: Tim How received fifth place ribbon and a gift certificate from McDonald's number 34.

claims.subject: Eric Owen
claims.claim: Eric Owen received fourth place ribbon and a gift certificate from McDonald's number 37.

claims.subject: Mike Turley
claims.claim: Mike Turley received the third place ribbon and a five dollar gift certificate from the didn't verspoke, number 41.

claims.subject: Mike Turley
claims.claim: Mike Turley will maintain the race Leeter jersey he wore this afternoon in the criterion.

claims.subject: Number 35
claims.claim: Number 35 received second place and a 25 dollar gift certificate from B&H sports.

claims.subject: Mike Constantine
claims.claim: Mike Constantine received second place and a 25 dollar gift certificate from B&H sports, number 33.

transcript.text: Receiving the 6th place ribbon and a gift certificate from McDonald's, number 38, Kyle Pounds.
In 5th place, also the winner of a preen, number 34, receiving a 5th place ribbon and a gift certificate from McDonald's, number 34, Tim Hogan.
And in 4th place, also receiving a gift certificate and ribbon, number 37, Eric Owen.
Now on to the awards blocks, receiving the 3rd place ribbon on the 3rd place position on the awards blocks, as well as a 5 dollar gift certificate from the Denver Spoke, number 41, Mike Turley.
Mike will maintain the race leader jersey that he wore this afternoon in the Criterion.
Now on to 2nd place, number 35, first place ribbon, as well as a 25 dollar gift certificate from B&H Sports, number 33, Mike Constantino.
Three salutes to the 10-11 boys, Brittany and Park Criterion.
And one more to the west.
All right, let's give him one final hand.
